title: Alexander Ross
text:
Email

I am a postdoctoral researcher at Université Laval. I received my PhD in Political Science from UC Riverside in 2024.
My research interests include personal debt, political behavior, and American politics.

title: Project Context: Academic Website for Alexander Ross
text:

GitHub Pages enabled (main branch, /docs folder)
Custom domain connected (DNS via WordPress.com)
HTTPS enabled

title: Research
text: Strategic Considerations and Support for Direct Democracy in the United States With Daniel R. Biggers. Electoral Studies, 2023. [DOI]

title: Research
text: Strategic Considerations and Support for Direct Democracy in the United States With Daniel R. Biggers. Electoral Studies, 2023. [DOI]

title: Research
text:
Current Project

PASI (Programme d’accompagnement et de soutien à l’intégration)
I am currently working at Université Laval on a project analyzing a novel panel dataset on recent immigrants to Quebec. This project, funded by MIFI (Quebec’s immigration government agency), surveys participants recruited through administrative data to study the impact of initial experiences on later political and social integration and political attitudes.
This is the first panel dataset on political attitudes composed exclusively of recently arrived immigrants in Canada or the US.

title: Research
text:
Working Papers & Conference Presentations
Deserving Debtors? The Determinants of Attitudes Toward Federal Student Loan Debtors Presented at APSA 2023 | Currently under review

Participating in Integration Activities and Participating in Quebec Society: A Study of Integration Programs’ Use and Impacts With Valérie-Anne Mahéo and Thomas Gareau-Paquette Presented at CPSA 2025

Yelp Stars and Partisan Stripes: Evidence of Affective Polarization in Online Business Reviews With Adam Hobbs and Loren Collingwood Forthcoming at MPSA 2026

Housing Shocks and Redistributive Preferences With Adam Hobbs Presented at MPSA 2024 & APSA 2025

Owed Gratitude? The Impact of Attempted Debt Cancelation on Attitudes and Behaviors Presented at APSA 2024

Debtors and Democracy: The Impact of Federal Student Loan Debt on Participation and Attitudes among Debtors Presented at MPSA 2023

Voting and Proactive Social Norm Compliance With Daniel R. Biggers Presented at APSA 2023

Dropbox Proximity and Voting Propensity With Loren Collingwood and Benjamin Gonzalez O’Brien

title: Website Update Cheat Sheet
text:
Every update follows this pattern:
cd /Users/alexanderross/Desktop/website
# 1. Make your edits (see below)
# 2. Render the site
quarto render
# 3. Push to GitHub
git add .
git commit -m "Brief description of change"
git push
Changes go live within 1-2 minutes.







Update your CV and save as Alex_CV.pdf
Copy it to files/Alex_CV.pdf (replace the old one)
Run the workflow above






Open projects.qmd in any text editor
Add under the ## Publications section:

**Paper Title Here**
With Coauthor Name. *Journal Name*, Year.
[[DOI]](https://doi.org/xxxxx)

Save and run the workflow above






Open projects.qmd
Add under ## Working Papers & Conference Presentations:

**Paper Title Here**
With Coauthor Name
*Presented at APSA 2025*

Save and run the workflow






Open index.qmd
Edit the text between :::{#hero-heading} and :::
Save and run the workflow






Open contact.qmd
Edit as needed
Save and run the workflow






Save new photo as headshot.jpeg
Copy to images/headshot.jpeg (replace old one)
Run the workflow






To see changes locally before pushing:
cd /Users/alexanderross/Desktop/website
quarto preview
Opens at http://localhost:4000 (or similar). Press Ctrl+C to stop.







File
What It Controls




index.qmd
Homepage, bio, education


projects.qmd

cv.qmd
CV page (embeds PDF)

files/Alex_CV.pdf

Your photo


_quarto.yml
Nav bar, site title, footer


custom.scss
Colors, fonts, styling







# See what changed
git status

# Undo all uncommitted changes
git checkout .

# See recent commits
git log --oneline -5
